stories.headline: Impeachment court to vote July 20 on subpoenaing Sara Duterte's bank and tax records
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court wrapped up oral arguments on whether to subpoena Vice President Sara Duterte's bank records, tax returns and Anti-Money Laundering Council documents linked to allegations of unexplained wealth, with Senate President Francis Escudero saying the senator-judges will vote on the request on July 20. Prosecutor Chel Diokno argued the court's power to examine the records flows from the Constitution, while the defense called the move a fishing expedition that would breach bank-secrecy laws. The prosecution said her financial transactions reached P208.15 million in 2007.
stories.context: The impeachment covers alleged misuse of confidential funds, unexplained wealth, and grave-threat allegations against President Marcos, the first lady and former Speaker Martin Romualdez. Examining sealed tax records also requires presidential approval under the tax code, which Malacanang said it has not been asked to give.
stories.watch_for: Senator-judges are set to vote on the subpoena request on July 20, with a simple majority of those present required. NBI Director Melvin Matibag is scheduled to testify on the grave-threats article on July 21.

stories.headline: Duterte's net satisfaction rating rises to 'good' +31 in latest SWS survey
stories.summary: Vice President Sara Duterte's net satisfaction rating rose to a 'good' +31 in the latest Social Weather Stations survey, with 58% of respondents satisfied and 26% dissatisfied. Her scores were highest in Mindanao, at an 'excellent' +84, and lowest in Metro Manila, at a 'neutral' +3, SWS said. The survey polled 1,200 adults nationwide from June 20 to 29.
stories.context: SWS classifies net satisfaction scores of +30 to +49 as 'good' and +70 or higher as 'excellent.' The survey was taken as Duterte faces an impeachment trial that could bar her from public office.

stories.headline: Marcos, Singapore's Wong agree to pursue new bilateral deals during working visit
stories.summary: President Marcos met Singapore Prime Minister Lawrence Wong at the Istana on July 15 and the two agreed to pursue new bilateral agreements, discussing expanded trade, investment, artificial intelligence, and cooperation on health and social development, according to Malacanang. The leaders reaffirmed a shared commitment to keeping ASEAN peaceful and stable. Marcos wrapped up the two-day working visit, which also included meetings with business leaders and investors, on July 16.
stories.context: The Philippines chairs ASEAN this year, with Singapore due to take over the chairmanship in 2027.

stories.headline: Marcos appoints orthopedic surgeon Jose Pujalte Jr. as new health secretary
stories.summary: President Marcos appointed Dr. Jose Pujalte Jr. as secretary of the Department of Health, replacing Teodoro Herbosa, who resigned while on medical leave after a knee replacement, GMA News reported. Pujalte has been with the department since 1991 and served as medical center chief of the Philippine Orthopedic Center from 2014. He earned his medical degree from the University of the Philippines and previously led the Philippine Orthopedic Association.

stories.headline: Metro Manila's record P85 daily minimum wage increase to roll out in two tranches
stories.summary: A P85 increase in Metro Manila's daily minimum wage, described by labor officials as the region's largest single adjustment, will take effect in two tranches, the Department of Labor and Employment said. A first P60 raise takes effect July 25 and a further P25 on January 20, 2027, bringing the region's minimum wage to P780. DOLE said implementation was moved from July 19 due to weather and publication requirements, and put compliance in the capital at about 94-95%.
stories.watch_for: The first P60 tranche takes effect July 25 and the remaining P25 on January 20, 2027. Wage boards for Calabarzon, Central Luzon and Central Visayas are still deliberating their own adjustments.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano keeps spewing lava on 192nd day of unrest; Alert Level 3 stays
stories.summary: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said Mayon Volcano in Albay produced fresh lava and ash bursts early on July 16, including an episode between 5:04 and 7:07 a.m., as it continued generating incandescent lava flows and rockfalls. Phivolcs kept the volcano at Alert Level 3, denoting a high level of unrest, and reiterated that entry within the six-kilometer permanent danger zone remains prohibited. The volcano has been restive for 192 days.

stories.headline: Mass burial held for nine victims of Sarangani landslide as search for infant continues
stories.summary: Nine of the ten people killed in a July 10 landslide in Barangay Poblacion, Malapatan, Sarangani were laid to rest in a mass burial on July 16, local officials said. The landslide struck around 3 a.m. after days of heavy rain in an area still recovering from a magnitude 7.8 earthquake on June 8, and the victims were related by blood or marriage, according to Mayor Salway Sumbo Jr. Search and retrieval efforts continue for a missing infant, and authorities are enforcing evacuations amid persistent rain.
stories.context: Sarangani and nearby areas have been hit by repeated disasters, including the June 8 magnitude 7.8 quake off Maasim and subsequent aftershocks.

stories.headline: Heat index hits 'danger' level of 49C in Infanta, Quezon
stories.summary: The state weather bureau PAGASA recorded a heat index of 49 degrees Celsius in Infanta, Quezon at 2 p.m. on July 16, within the 'danger' range where prolonged exposure can cause heat cramps, heat exhaustion and heat stroke. Authorities advised residents to drink water regularly, wear light clothing, limit sun exposure between 10 a.m. and 4 p.m., and avoid strenuous outdoor activity during peak heat.

stories.headline: Southwest monsoon brings isolated rains to northern Luzon; no active cyclone
stories.summary: PAGASA said the southwest monsoon, or habagat, was affecting extreme northern Luzon as of the afternoon of July 16, bringing isolated rain showers and thunderstorms over Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Abra, Batanes and the Babuyan Islands. The rest of the country can expect localized thunderstorms, with possible flash floods or landslides during heavier downpours. No tropical cyclone was active inside the Philippine Area of Responsibility.

stories.headline: DOJ to help families of Tacloban school shooting victims claim government aid
stories.summary: The Department of Justice said its regional prosecution office in Eastern Visayas is helping families of a Tacloban school shooting file for financial assistance through the Board of Claims, with three people killed and three others hurt. A minor identified only as 'Rod' faces preliminary investigation for three counts of murder and two of attempted murder, while a 14-year-old is in a youth rehabilitation center under social welfare intervention, DOJ Undersecretary Ian Norman Dato said.

stories.headline: Cebu's Grace Quintanilla takes six-shot lead at Singapore Amateur Open
stories.summary: Filipino golfer Grace Quintanilla of Cebu opened a six-stroke lead in the women's division of the Singapore Amateur Open after a third-round 70 for a 54-hole total of 208 at the Singapore Island Country Club, with Singapore's Amelie Ng second at 214. In the men's division, Filipino Rolando Bregente was tied for fourth at three strokes back, while Australia's Jonty Lunzon led at 200.

stories.headline: Cayetano challenges government to probe 2019 SEA Games as Senate minority eyes flood-control projects
stories.summary: Senator Alan Peter Cayetano said he welcomed a full investigation into the 2019 Southeast Asian Games and proposed that it proceed alongside the Senate minority's planned scrutiny of flood-control projects. His remarks followed NBI Director Melvin Matibag's announcement of a task force to examine alleged irregularities in the Games' bidding and funding, citing new documents. Cayetano chaired the 2019 Games' organizing committee, PHISGOC.
stories.context: Matibag is also a prosecution witness in Vice President Sara Duterte's impeachment trial, and some senators questioned whether his SEA Games remarks were meant to intimidate. Flood-control spending has been under scrutiny following a corruption case in the public works department.

stories.headline: Marcos tells agencies to include only fast-implementing projects in 2027 budget
stories.summary: President Marcos directed government agencies to include only projects that can be implemented quickly and without obstacles in the 2027 national budget, Malacanang said, adding that funding should prioritize programs that directly benefit the public and align with the national development plan. The instruction followed Marcos's approval of the 2027 National Expenditure Program on July 10.

stories.headline: More than 500 Rohingya feared dead after two boats sink off Myanmar
stories.summary: More than 500 people, most of them Rohingya, are feared dead or missing after two boats that left Myanmar's Rakhine State in late June sank, UN agencies said. One vessel carrying about 250 people lost contact soon after departure, while a second with around 280 aboard reportedly sank off the Ayeyarwady coast on July 8, according to the UN refugee agency and the International Organization for Migration. The agencies said heavy rain and flooding had made the crossings especially dangerous.
stories.context: The Rohingya, a mostly Muslim minority, have fled Myanmar in large numbers; roughly 1.2 million stateless Rohingya live in overcrowded camps in Bangladesh. UN agencies say sea crossings from the region have become increasingly deadly.

stories.headline: TSMC pledges additional $100 billion for US chip plants amid AI demand
stories.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. announced an additional $100 billion investment in the United States to build advanced chipmaking and packaging plants in Arizona, citing strong demand driven by artificial intelligence, Nikkei Asia reported. The world's largest contract chipmaker also raised its full-year capital spending forecast to $64 billion and projected about 40% revenue growth for the year.

stories.headline: Protests erupt in Kyiv after Zelenskyy dismisses defense minister
stories.summary: Hundreds of people protested in central Kyiv, with demonstrations also reported in Lviv, Odesa and Dnipro, after President Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, Al Jazeera reported. Fedorov, a former digital transformation minister credited with reforming defense procurement, was among several officials replaced in a cabinet reshuffle that also installed Sergiy Koretsky as prime minister. Interior Minister Ihor Klymenko is expected to become the new defense minister.
